item.title: Czechs Look Past Energy Shock for Now With Rates Set to Stay Put
item.description: Czech policymakers are likely to keep interest rates unchanged, taking more time to gauge the broader impact of more expensive fuels on living costs and economic growth.

item.title: Goldman’s Singapore Co-Head and Key Engineering Exec Leaves Firm
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s co-head of Singapore and a key engineering executive is leaving the firm, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: EU Fails to Finalize US Trade Deal Despite Trump’s Demands
item.description: The European Union failed to finalize a long-delayed US trade deal during overnight talks, despite warnings from President Donald Trump that he would soon impose fresh tariffs.

item.title: Philippine Peso Set for Best Daily Gain in a Month on Oil Relief
item.description: The Philippine peso is headed for its best gain in a month as optimism over a US-Iran peace deal boosts prospects for further declines in oil prices.

item.title: Yen Intervention Rally Faces High Hurdle at 155 to the Dollar
item.description: The yen’s failure to breach 155 to the dollar after repeated cases of suspected intervention by Japan is raising questions over the durability of the currency’s recent advance.

item.title: OceanaGold Hunts for More Deals to Add to Strong Growth Pipeline
item.description: Canadian gold miner OceanaGold Corp. is aggressively canvassing options to buy other producers and existing operations while also pursuing a strong organic growth strategy, with two new mines in development.

item.title: Skyroot Rockets to Unicorn Status Backed by GIC, BlackRock Funds
item.description: Skyroot Aerospace Pvt. has become India’s first space-tech unicorn after securing $60 million in fresh funding from GIC Pte and Sherpalo Ventures, lifting its valuation to $1.1 billion.

item.title: Mbappé Will Power France to World Cup Gold Over Spain, BofA Says
item.description: France will win this year’s FIFA World Cup and claim its third title by beating Spain in the final, Bank of America Corp. analysts predicted.

item.title: European Industry Is in Crosshairs of China’s New Five-Year Plan
item.description: For Europe, a new cycle of Chinese rivalry heralded by its latest roadmap for advancement is forcing soul searching on how to respond.

item.title: Oil and Stocks Depend on How Serious Trump Deal Is
item.description: Markets care far more about the Strait than Iran’s nuclear program.

item.title: Africa’s Newest Oil Refinery Fires Up to Supply Fuel for Angola
item.description: Angola’s Cabinda oil refinery, among the first to be built in Africa in decades, has started shipping fuel, helping the crude producer bolster domestic supply and reduce reliance on imports.

item.title: The Bank of England Joins Those Who Doubt UK Growth Figures
item.description: Figures next week are likely to paint a rosy picture for the UK economy in the first quarter. The trouble is that fewer and fewer people believe them.

item.title: Alibaba Shares Outpace Tencent’s as Chip Exposure Fuels Demand
item.description: The torrid stock rally in Asian chipmakers is driving a divergence between China’s two internet giants, with Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. gaining an edge over rival Tencent Holdings Ltd. due to investor enthusiasm about its ambitious semiconductor unit.

item.title: AI Boom Trumps Sleep, Says Boss of Data Center Operator NEXTDC
item.description: Short on sleep but flush with new funds, the head of Australian data center operator NEXTDC Ltd. has a message to investors: You snooze, you lose.

item.title: Dry-Bulk Shipping Rates Hit Two-Year High on Capesize Demand
item.description: A key measure of bulk-shipping rates jumped to the highest level since December 2023, driven by rising demand for Capesize vessels along with tightening supply of ships that haul bulk commodities.

item.title: Kimi Chatbot Maker Moonshot AI Valued at $20 Billion in Meituan-Led Round
item.description: Moonshot AI has raised about $2 billion in its latest funding round, signaling growing investor appetite for Chinese startups rivaling Silicon Valley’s leaders.

item.title: LME Sees Hong Kong Sheds Swelling to Hundreds of Thousands Tons
item.description: The London Metal Exchange is ready to expand its suite of approved warehouses in Hong Kong after licensing the first last year, according to Chief Executive Officer Matthew Chamberlain.

item.title: Australia’s War-Driven Fuel Costs Swing Trade Into Deficit
item.description: Australia recorded its first trade deficit in more than eight years as the war in Iran drove up the cost of fuel imports, squeezing households and further fueling inflationary pressures that have forced the Reserve Bank to raise interest rates.

item.title: Top Trump Aide Says Administration Won’t Pick Winners in AI Race
item.description: White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles said the US government would refrain from choosing winners and losers in artificial intelligence, the latest signal from a top aide to President Donald Trump as his administration prepares new AI policy directives.

item.title: Bessent to Meet Takaichi, Katayama, Ueda in Japan Next Week
item.description: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent will spend three days in Japan starting Monday and is expected to meet senior officials including Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi before traveling to China, according to local media reports.

item.title: Earnings Beats Offer India Markets a Silver Lining Amid Oil Shock
item.description: Strong domestic demand is helping companies post better-than-expected results.

item.title: World’s Best-Performing Market to Get Lift From US Retail Money
item.description: South Korean stocks are set for a further boost as Interactive Brokers Group Inc. gives US retail investors direct access to the world’s hottest market.

item.title: Colombia’s Surprise Rate Hold Aimed to Avoid Election Noise
item.description: Colombia’s central bank said its surprise decision to hold interest rates steady in late April was meant to avoid perceptions that it was interfering in upcoming presidential elections.

item.title: Chinese Consumers Open Wallets as Malaise Lifted During Holidays
item.description: Chinese households picked up their pace of spending over the recent Labor Day extended holiday weekend, according to preliminary figures economists are assessing for a turnaround from a disappointing March.

item.title: Philippine Growth Slumps to 2.8% in Challenge for Marcos
item.description: Philippine growth unexpectedly slowed in the first quarter, leaving the country a laggard within the region and challenging policymakers trying to cool inflation and support the peso.

item.title: Hedge Fund SPX Capital Faces Shake-Up, Moves to Exit London
item.description: Brazilian hedge fund SPX Capital is undergoing a major restructuring, with senior partners departing and a rethink of operations that will include shutting its London office, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Korea Surpasses Canada as World’s Seventh-Largest Stock Market
item.description: South Korea’s equity market has overtaken Canada’s as the world’s seventh largest, propelled by insatiable demand for chips powering artificial intelligence.

item.title: Montage Tops CATL as Priciest Dual-Listed Stock After Chip Rally
item.description: Montage Technology Co. has overtaken Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. as the most expensive dual-listed stock in Hong Kong relative to its mainland shares, propelled by surging demand for AI chips.

item.title: RBNZ’s Frequent Remit Adjustments Risk Policy Error, OECD Warns
item.description: New Zealand should reconsider how frequently it adjusts the Reserve Bank’s remit because it raises the risk of monetary policy mistakes, according to the Organisation for Economic Cooperation and Development.

item.title: Milei Ally Demands Answers to Graft Allegations Against Top Aide
item.description: Argentine President Javier Milei’s cabinet chief was urged to disclose his finances and real estate assets by the libertarian leader’s senior ally in Congress, in the first public sign of friction within the ruling coalition over a growing corruption scandal.
